verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 encodes the type 1 inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor, a large (~2700 aa) tetrameric calcium-release channel whose six transmembrane helices are embedded exclusively in the ER membrane. The entire ligand-binding and regulatory apparatus faces the cytosol, and Ca2+ is released from the ER lumen to the cytosol upon IP3 binding. There is no plasma membrane topology, GPI anchor, outer-leaflet lipidation, or documented surface pool. Pre-no checklist: no cell-surface-directed therapeutic program targets ITPR1 on the cell surface; it is not a TM proligand subject to ectodomain shedding; no wash-resistant tethering to a PM scaffold is documented; no minority PM pool or trafficking cycling to the PM is reported; no cell-death-, cancer-, activation-, or germline-induced outer-leaflet display has been documented for this protein specifically; it is not a lysosomal TM protein; no dual-localization PM pool is described. All five contextual buckets are ruled out by absence of gene-specific experimental evidence of any outer-leaflet presence.
